reasoning: Confirmed real. export_orders_bulk_action_process() at lines 593-634 contains zero current_user_can() calls — verified by exhaustive grep of the entire plugin source. The function is registered on handle_bulk_actions-edit-shop_order and handle_bulk_actions-woocommerce_page_wc-orders (constructor lines 47-58). WordPress core fires the handle_bulk_actions-{screen} filter after verifying the WP nonce (_wpnonce) but does not enforce any role/capability itself before calling the filter. The only capability checks in the plugin are: add_menu() line 238-239 (manage_woocommerce), ajax_gate() line 514 (view_woocommerce_reports OR export_woocommerce_orders custom cap), and user_can_add_custom_php() line 688. None guard the bulk handler. The plugin defines a custom capability self::$cap_export_orders = 'export_woocommerce_orders' and ajax_gate() enforces it, but the bulk action path bypasses it entirely. A user who has WooCommerce orders-list access (e.g. granted view_woocommerce_reports but not export_woocommerce_orders) can submit a crafted bulk action POST to mark or unmark any order's export metadata. Reachability is accurate: the gate is page-level access to edit-shop_order/wc-orders, which is shop_manager+ by default but can be widened via the filterable woe_current_user_can_export hook (line 238). The claimed severity of medium is appropriate — the impact is tampering with order export metadata (woe_order_exported), not order data itself, and requires authentication with at least WooCommerce orders-list access.
